metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4111
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig3: Micro-LC-MS/MS analysis identified dynamin-1 as an interacting partner of snx9 and was validated by GST pull-down assay. Immunocytochemistry further confirmed the interaction between snx9 and dynamin-1.

Fig4A-C: SynaptopHluorin, a VAMP-2/synaptobrevin-2 fused with a pH-sensitive variant of GFP was used to check if the interaction between snx9 and dynamin1 could be involved in SV endocytosis. The fluorescence of spH is quenched once synaptic vesicles are endocytosed and re-acidified (from external pH ~7.4 to an internal pH of a synaptic vesicle ~5.5) and reliably reflects the kinetics of endocytosis. To investigate the effect of SNX9 on endocytosis, FLAG-tagged SNX9 or truncated SNX9 variants were cotransfected with spH into hippocampal neurons. Upon electrical stimulation (600 actions potentials/20 Hz), the fluorescence intensity of individual spH boutons increased rapidly, reached a peak, and then decayed with an exponential time course. In boutons expressing SNX9, synaptic vesicle endocytosis occurred much more slowly than in non-expressing boutons.
Fig 4D: Histograms of the rates of spH fluorescence decay demonstrate that SNX9 overexpression significantly slows SV endocytosis than control.
Fig 5A: An alternative method of measuring SV endocytosis involved employing FM 4-64, a red-shifting fluorescent lipophilic dye with the same protocol as in for Fig4.
Fig 5B-C: The intensity of FM 4-64 staining of SNX9- or SNX9-SH3-transfected synaptic boutons were invariably higher than that of the control boutons confirming that endocytosis proceeds more slowly when SNX9 was overexpressed.
Fig 8D-E: Co-transfecting neurons with Snx9 siRNA constructs and spH demonstrated a similar slowing of SV endocytosis.
Fig 8A-C: The efficiency of Snx9 SiRNA constructs was verified by staining with the anti-snx9 antibody. The expression of snx9 was significantly lowered in neurons transfected with siRNA constructs than control. The expression of other endocytic proteins such as syndapin, dynamin, endophilin, and Rab5b was not affected.

The authors claim that overexpression of SNX9 may act in a dominant-negative manner to prevent dynamin or N-WASP from interacting with other endocytic proteins. While silencing the expression of SNX9 could affect its physiological role in endocytosis. Overall, Snx9 seems to impair SV endocytosis in neurons.. Experimental description: E-18 primary rat hippocampal neurons were prepared from Sprague-Dawley as described in PMID: 11477424.
